breaking.title: Chud the Builder’s bond revoked after new footage emerges in Tennessee shooting case
breaking.summary: Chud the Builder’s bond in a Davidson County misdemeanor case was revoked after investigators presented footage that allegedly shows him shooting Joshua Fox before being attacked. The evidence challenges his self-defense claim in the May 2026 courthouse shooting. With serious felony charges already pending, Eatherly remains in jail as both cases move through the court system.

breaking.title: After protests, three injured Kukis shifted out of Imphal hospital
breaking.summary: The three youths were moved 60 km away to Churachandpur Medical College under tight security; protesters had earlier tried to storm RIMS and take away the trio who were injured in an attack on their village in Kuki-majority Kangpokpi

breaking.title: West Asia LIVE: Trump, Iran’s President sign deal to end West Asia war
breaking.summary: Pakistan says that the deal to end the war in Iran is taking immediate effect' after both sides have signed it

breaking.title: Global Market Today: Asian shares gain as US-Iran deal takes effect
breaking.summary: US stock futures rose as President Trump signed an interim deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing energy concerns. This comes as the Federal Reserve signaled potential rate hikes to combat inflation, causing bond yields to climb and the yen to weaken.

breaking.title: FPIs pump record funds into G-Secs after policy shift
breaking.summary: Foreign funds are pouring into India's government bond market at a record pace this month, driven by tax exemptions on interest and capital gains, a wider investment basket, and relaxed limits. These measures, coupled with a stable rupee and calmer geopolitics, have spurred significant inflows, with continued momentum dependent on global factors and potential inclusion in major bond indices.

breaking.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
breaking.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

breaking.title: South Korea to ease civilian curbs along border with North
breaking.summary: The two Koreas remain technically at war because their 1950-53 conflict ended in an armistice
